title: How many people have been killed in the US-Israel war on Iran?
pages_or_sections: Israel section, lines 185-196 in opened page; plus latest Reuters snippets on 11 March 2026
strengths: Provides current, side-attributed conflict context and confirms the all-Israel civilian death count still stood at 11 as of 10-11 March 2026.
limitations: Not strike-footprint specific; nationwide total is not suitable by itself for a subnational DR denominator.
notes: Key contextual figure: 11 civilians killed in Israel, including 9 in the Beit Shemesh strike on 1 March 2026.

title: In one Israeli city, 3 siblings are among those killed in the war against Iran
date_scope: 1 March 2026 strike aftermath reported on 5 March 2026
strengths: Strike-specific reporting that identifies three under-18 victims among the nine dead.
limitations: Secondary reporting based partly on Israeli rescue-service information; not a formal casualty registry.
notes: AP states that three children aged about 17, 15, and 13 were among nine killed in the Iranian missile strike.

title: Emergency in Israel: ongoing updates
author_or_org: Magen David Adom UK / MDA-linked operational updates
pages_or_sections: Beit Shemesh operational update, lines 510-513 and 556-558
strengths: Provides strike-specific casualty handling figures from rescue-service reporting.
limitations: Advocacy/operational web page rather than a formal statistical report; some update lines differ slightly as the rescue operation evolved.
notes: Key strike summary: 65 casualties to hospitals including 9 fatalities.

title: List of localities in Israel with population data
date_scope: 31 December reference year in the locality table
strengths: Official Israeli population table suitable for a municipal fallback denominator.
limitations: Population is not specific to the impacted block and may predate the 2026 strike by several years.
